title: announce: app_data TLV decoding cannot handle multiple concatenated types
description:
Files: python/src/lichen/announce/coords.py, python/src/lichen/announce/processor.py:233-234

The app_data decode functions (decode_coords, decode_congestion, decode_dtn_expiry, decode_dtn_pending) only check if the FIRST byte matches their type code:

```python
def decode_coords(app_data: bytes) -> tuple[float, float] | None:
    if app_data[0] != APP_DATA_TYPE_COORDS:
        return None  # Only checks first byte
```

In processor.py, both decoders are called on the same app_data:

```python
coords = decode_coords(announce.app_data)
congestion = decode_congestion(announce.app_data)
```

Problem: If app_data contains coords (9 bytes) followed by congestion (2 bytes), only coords will be decoded. The congestion decoder sees 0x01 (COORDS type) as the first byte and returns None.

Either:
1. The protocol design should document that app_data can only contain ONE type at a time, OR
2. The decode functions need to scan through app_data looking for their type (requires TLV with length field), OR  
3. A composite decode function that parses all TLVs at once

Current behavior silently drops data if multiple types are concatenated.
